sections.question: There is a dispute within our homeowners' association regarding the propriety of a candidate making a political statement under the guise of the President's message of the homeowners association newsletter. The candidate/association president states he checked with the FPPC office prior to publication and was assured the statement did not violate FPPC guidelines, while others understand a prior FPPC staff opinion indicated the association had become an unwilling political action committee because of the use of the word 'support.' The requestor seeks guidance on whether such political messages in homeowners association newsletters violate FPPC guidelines and how they affect the association's tax-exempt status.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a political statement made by a candidate/homeowners association president in the association's newsletter violate FPPC guidelines, and what are the implications for the organization's legal status?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a homeowners association member, not an FPPC response. Document contains conflicting accounts of prior FPPC staff guidance. No formal question/conclusion sections present. References an attached newsletter statement but attachment not included in text provided. Minor OCR errors present (e.g., 'homeorazner's' for 'homeowners').

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: There is a dispute within our homeowners' association regarding the propriety of a candidate making a political statement under the guise of the President's message of the homeowners association newsletter. The candidate/association president states he checked with the FPPC office prior to publication and was assured the statement did not violate FPPC guidelines, while others understand a prior FPPC staff opinion indicated the association had become an unwilling political action committee because of the use of the word 'support.' The requestor seeks guidance on whether such political messages in homeowners association newsletters violate FPPC guidelines and how they affect the association's tax-exempt status.

embedding.summary: Violet Hembrow requests FPPC guidance regarding a dispute within her homeowners association about whether the association president/candidate's political statement in the association newsletter violates FPPC guidelines and jeopardizes the organization's tax-exempt status.
